channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17635] LFSCK doesn't detect missing LMV hash
item.description:
I see a 2.15.3-based system in a similar to LU-16717 but not exactly the same state, an interrupted mdt->mdt migration left a directory in an inaccessible state:

# lfs getdirstripe .
lmv_stripe_count: 3 lmv_stripe_offset: 0 lmv_hash_type: none,migrating
mdtidx FID[seq:oid:ver]
0 [0x20002f9d0:0x6a:0x0]
1 [0x240025042:0x62:0x0]
1 [0x240025064:0x8e32:0x0]
# touch test
touch: cannot touch 'test': Bad file descriptor

unlike the LU-16717 case, the directory has not "bad_type" flag and the flag did not appear after an LFSCK run. So I think the patches from LU-16717 would not help as the checks inside them need "bad_type" flag to be set.
item.summary: LFSCK doesn't detect missing LMV hash

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55364/
Subject: LU-17635 lfsck: detect missing LMV hash
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 57b992fceb593942b9ea6facd88cbda4db9786e9

Here is my attempt to simulate fs issue with a mssing LMV hash (I had to interrupt a migration of /mnt/lustre/testdir ):

[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs path2fid /mnt/lustre/testdir
[0x240000402:0x15:0x0]
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs getdirstripe /mnt/lustre/testdir
lmv_stripe_count: 2 lmv_stripe_offset: 1 lmv_hash_type: crush,migrating,fixed
mdtidx		 FID[seq:oid:ver]
     1		 [0x240000400:0x4:0x0]		
     0		 [0x200000402:0x1:0x0]		
[root@rocky tests]# 
[root@rocky tests]# umount /mnt/lustre-mds1/
[root@rocky tests]# umount /mnt/lustre-mds2/
[root@rocky tests]# mount -t ldiskfs /dev/mapper/mds1_flakey /mnt/lustre-mds1/
[root@rocky tests]# mount -t ldiskfs /dev/mapper/mds2_flakey /mnt/lustre-mds2/
[root@rocky tests]# 
[root@rocky tests]# #no, migrating
[root@rocky tests]# setfattr -n trusted.lmv -v 0s0AzSDAIAAAABAAAAAAAAgAEAAAABAAAAAgAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA= /mnt/lustre-mds2/REMOTE_PARENT_DIR/0x240000402\:0x15\:0x0/
[root@rocky tests]# setfattr -n trusted.lmv -v 0s0AzUDAIAAAAAAAAAAAAAgAEAAAABAAAAAgAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=  /mnt/lustre-mds2/REMOTE_PARENT_DIR/0x240000402\:0x15\:0x0/\[0x240000400\:0x4\:0x0\]\:0/
[root@rocky tests]# setfattr -n trusted.lmv -v 0s0AzUDAIAAAABAAAAAAAAgAEAAAABAAAAAgAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA= /mnt/lustre-mds1/REMOTE_PARENT_DIR/0x200000402\:0x1\:0x0/
[root@rocky tests]# 
[root@rocky tests]# 
[root@rocky tests]# umount /mnt/lustre-mds1/
[root@rocky tests]# umount /mnt/lustre-mds2/
[root@rocky tests]# mount -t lustre /dev/mapper/mds1_flakey /mnt/lustre-mds1/
[root@rocky tests]# mount -t lustre /dev/mapper/mds2_flakey /mnt/lustre-mds2/
[root@rocky tests]# 
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs getdirstripe /mnt/lustre/testdir
lmv_stripe_count: 2 lmv_stripe_offset: 1 lmv_hash_type: none,migrating
mdtidx		 FID[seq:oid:ver]
     1		 [0x240000400:0x4:0x0]		
     0		 [0x200000402:0x1:0x0]		
[root@rocky tests]# 
[root@rocky tests]# 
(failed reverse-i-search)`': mount -t lustre /dev/mapper/mds2_flakey /mnt/lustre-^Cs2/
[root@rocky tests]# rm -rf /mnt/lustre/testdir/
rm: cannot remove '/mnt/lustre/testdir/': Is a directory
[root@rocky tests]# rm -rf /mnt/lustre/testdir/
[root@rocky tests]# rm -rf /mnt/lustre/testdir/
[root@rocky tests]# rmdir /mnt/lustre/testdir/
rmdir: failed to remove '/mnt/lustre/testdir/': Invalid argument
[root@rocky tests]# ls /mnt/lustre/testdir/
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lctl lfsck_start -M lustre-MDT0001 -t namespace
Started LFSCK on the device lustre-MDT0001: scrub namespace
[root@rocky tests]# ../utils/lfs getdirstripe /mnt/lustre/testdir
lmv_stripe_count: 2 lmv_stripe_offset: 1 lmv_hash_type: fnv_1a_64,migrating
mdtidx		 FID[seq:oid:ver]
     1		 [0x240000400:0x4:0x0]		
     0		 [0x200000402:0x1:0x0]		
[root@rocky tests]# rmdir /mnt/lustre/testdir/
[root@rocky tests]# ls /mnt/lustre/
[root@rocky tests]#

As you can see, patched LFSCK fixes the issue.

"Alexander Zarochentsev <alexander.zarochentsev@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55364
Subject: LU-17635 lfsck: detect missing LMV hash
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 6a6409156b55659b78d8e672ac45a214d9a1f101
